'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987年}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002年}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** * Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2qq1 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto, Nagano]] [[Category:Empun (circular tumuli)]] [[ja:桜ヶ丘古墳]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳}}

'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987年}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002年}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** * Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2qq1 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto, Nagano]] [[Category:Empun (circular tumuli)]] [[ja:桜ヶ丘古墳]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳}}

'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987年}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002年}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** * Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2qq1 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto, Nagano]] [[Category:Empun (circular tumuli)]] [[ja:桜ヶ丘古墳]] Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳}}

'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987年}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002年}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** * Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). '''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987年}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002年}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2111 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto, Nagano]] [[Category:Empun (circular tumuli)]] [[ja:桜ヶ丘古墳]] Horned Kabuto 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳}}

'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. '''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954年}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987年}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002年}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - '''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year }} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year }}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955年}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987年}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988年}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002nd year }}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - '''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year }} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year }}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year }}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year }}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year }}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year }} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year }} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002nd year }}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - '''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year }} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year }}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year }}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year }}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year }}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year }}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year }}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year }} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year }} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002nd year }}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year }} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2111 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto, Nagano]] [[Category:Empun (circular tumuli)]] [[ja:桜ヶ丘古墳]] Horned Kabuto 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳}}

'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year }} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year }}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). revision.text:
'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝|12=draft}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|12=draft}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|12=draft}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品|12=draft}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠|12=draft}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑|12=draft}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|12=draft}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|12=draft}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|12=draft}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|12=draft}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|12=draft}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|12=draft}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|12=draft}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|12=draft}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002nd year|12=draft}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|12=draft}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|12=draft}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館|12=draft}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2111 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation Horned Kabuto 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳|12=draft}}

Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2111 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation Horned Kabuto 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|竪穴式石室|" to "|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|"
revision.text:
'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|12=draft|12=draft}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品|12=draft|12=draft}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠|12=draft|12=draft}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑|12=draft|12=draft}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|12=draft|12=draft}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|12=draft|12=draft}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|12=draft|12=draft}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2111 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation Horned Kabuto 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:桜ヶ丘古墳 up to rev 101551196
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 桜ヶ丘古墳 |画像 = |別名 = 桜ケ丘古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[長野県]][[松本市]]浅間温泉1315（字飯治洞） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 41.45 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 59|経度秒 = 24.55 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径30m
高さ5m |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]] |出土品 = 天冠・甲冑ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 出土品（長野県宝） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 桜ヶ丘古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''桜ヶ丘古墳'''（さくらがおかこふん、桜ケ丘古墳）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[浅間温泉]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。出土品は[[長野県指定文化財一覧#県宝|長野県宝]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 長野県中部、[[松本盆地]]北東縁の桜ヶ丘丘陵の尾根先端部（標高約678メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）に中学生によって発見され、[[1955年]]（昭和30年）に[[発掘調査]]が実施されて[[副葬品]]が出土している。 [[墳]]形は円形で、直径約30メートル・高さ約5メートルを測る{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。埋葬施設は遺存状態が悪いが、[[竪穴式石室]]の一種で、主室・副室に分かれている{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。主室の内法は長さ2.5メートル・幅1.2-1.3メートル、副室（礫槨）の内法は長さ1.8-1.85メートル・幅約0.6メートルを測る{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。[[石室]]内からは金銅製天[[冠]]・[[甲冑]]一式を始めとする多数の副葬品が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。政治的な権威を示す天冠・竪櫛と、武力的な権威を示す武具・武器類が共に副葬される点で特異性を示す古墳になる。 出土品は[[2010年]]（[[平成]]22年）に長野県宝に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）、女鳥羽中学校生徒によって発見。 * [[1955年]]（昭和30年）、発掘調査。副葬品の出土（[[國學院大學]]の[[大場磐雄]]ら、[[1966年]]に報告）。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）、金銅製天冠が長野県宝に指定。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、県史編纂事業に伴う墳丘測量調査および遺物の一部の再実測（滝沢誠ら、1988年に報告）。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）3月17日、金銅製天冠以外の出土品が松本市指定重要文化財に指定。 * [[2002年]]度（[[平成]]14年度）、遺物再整理（松本市教育委員会、[[2003年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。 * [[2010年]]（平成22年）10月18日、出土品が長野県宝に指定。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|金銅製天冠（長野県宝）}}{{small|松本市立考古博物館展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|甲冑（長野県宝）}}]] 1955年（昭和30年）の発掘調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り[https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html 桜ヶ丘古墳出土品]（松本市ホームページ）。。 * 装身具類 ** 金銅製天冠 1 ** 黒漆塗竹製竪櫛 1 - 天冠に付着。 ** 瑪瑙製勾玉 1 ** ガラス製丸玉 9 ** ガラス製小玉 32 ** 滑石製臼玉 5 * 武具類 ** 三角板革綴衝角付胄 1 ** 革綴頸甲 1 ** 長方板革綴短甲 1 * 武器類 ** 鉄剣 5 ** 直刀 1 ** 鉄矛 1 ** 鉄鏃 5 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|衝角付冑 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|短甲 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|装身具・武器・頸甲 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 長野県宝（有形文化財） ** 桜ヶ丘古墳出土品（考古資料） - 2010年（平成22年）10月18日指定。 == 関連施設 == * [[松本市立考古博物館]]（松本市中山） - 桜ヶ丘古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=桜ヶ丘古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|桜ヶ丘古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=桜ケ丘古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|桜ケ丘古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/7551|title=長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 -再整理報告書-（松本市文化財調査報告No.170）|publisher=松本市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1966|chapter=|url=|title=信濃浅間古墳|publisher=本郷村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=青木繁夫|title=松本市桜ヶ丘古墳出土金銅天冠の修復処置（受託研究報告第40号）|date=1976-3-31|journal=保存科学|publisher=東京国立文化財研究所|volume=|number=15|pages=51-55|url=https://www.tobunken.go.jp/ccr/pdf/15/pdf/01507.pdf|naid=|doi=|ref=}} - リンクは東京文化財研究所。 * {{Cite journal|和書|author=滝沢誠|title=長野県松本市桜ケ丘古墳の再調査|date=1988-10|journal=信濃|publisher=信濃史学会|volume=40|number=10|pages=941-954|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sakuragaoka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html 桜ヶ丘古墳出土品] - 松本市ホームページ * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2111 桜ヶ丘古墳出土品] - 公益財団法人八十二文化財団 {{デフォルトソート:さくらかおかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[桜ヶ丘古墳]] to [[Sakuragaoka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 桜ヶ丘古墳 |画像 = |別名 = 桜ケ丘古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[長野県]][[松本市]]浅間温泉1315（字飯治洞） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 41.45 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 59|経度秒 = 24.55 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径30m
高さ5m |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]] |出土品 = 天冠・甲冑ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 出土品（長野県宝） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 桜ヶ丘古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''桜ヶ丘古墳'''（さくらがおかこふん、桜ケ丘古墳）は、[[長野県]][[松本市]][[浅間温泉]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。出土品は[[長野県指定文化財一覧#県宝|長野県宝]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 長野県中部、[[松本盆地]]北東縁の桜ヶ丘丘陵の尾根先端部（標高約678メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）に中学生によって発見され、[[1955年]]（昭和30年）に[[発掘調査]]が実施されて[[副葬品]]が出土している。 [[墳]]形は円形で、直径約30メートル・高さ約5メートルを測る{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。埋葬施設は遺存状態が悪いが、[[竪穴式石室]]の一種で、主室・副室に分かれている{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。主室の内法は長さ2.5メートル・幅1.2-1.3メートル、副室（礫槨）の内法は長さ1.8-1.85メートル・幅約0.6メートルを測る{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。[[石室]]内からは金銅製天[[冠]]・[[甲冑]]一式を始めとする多数の副葬品が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。政治的な権威を示す天冠・竪櫛と、武力的な権威を示す武具・武器類が共に副葬される点で特異性を示す古墳になる。 出土品は[[2010年]]（[[平成]]22年）に長野県宝に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）、女鳥羽中学校生徒によって発見。 * [[1955年]]（昭和30年）、発掘調査。副葬品の出土（[[國學院大學]]の[[大場磐雄]]ら、[[1966年]]に報告）。 * [[1969年]]（昭和44年）、金銅製天冠が長野県宝に指定。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、県史編纂事業に伴う墳丘測量調査および遺物の一部の再実測（滝沢誠ら、1988年に報告）。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）3月17日、金銅製天冠以外の出土品が松本市指定重要文化財に指定。 * [[2002年]]度（[[平成]]14年度）、遺物再整理（松本市教育委員会、[[2003年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}。 * [[2010年]]（平成22年）10月18日、出土品が長野県宝に指定。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|金銅製天冠（長野県宝）}}{{small|松本市立考古博物館展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|甲冑（長野県宝）}}]] 1955年（昭和30年）の発掘調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り[https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html 桜ヶ丘古墳出土品]（松本市ホームページ）。。 * 装身具類 ** 金銅製天冠 1 ** 黒漆塗竹製竪櫛 1 - 天冠に付着。 ** 瑪瑙製勾玉 1 ** ガラス製丸玉 9 ** ガラス製小玉 32 ** 滑石製臼玉 5 * 武具類 ** 三角板革綴衝角付胄 1 ** 革綴頸甲 1 ** 長方板革綴短甲 1 * 武器類 ** 鉄剣 5 ** 直刀 1 ** 鉄矛 1 ** 鉄鏃 5 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|衝角付冑 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|短甲 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|装身具・武器・頸甲 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 長野県宝（有形文化財） ** 桜ヶ丘古墳出土品（考古資料） - 2010年（平成22年）10月18日指定。 == 関連施設 == * [[松本市立考古博物館]]（松本市中山） - 桜ヶ丘古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=桜ヶ丘古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|桜ヶ丘古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=桜ケ丘古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|桜ケ丘古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2003|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/7551|title=長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 -再整理報告書-（松本市文化財調査報告No.170）|publisher=松本市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|長野県松本市桜ヶ丘古墳 再整理報告書|2003}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1966|chapter=|url=|title=信濃浅間古墳|publisher=本郷村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=青木繁夫|title=松本市桜ヶ丘古墳出土金銅天冠の修復処置（受託研究報告第40号）|date=1976-3-31|journal=保存科学|publisher=東京国立文化財研究所|volume=|number=15|pages=51-55|url=https://www.tobunken.go.jp/ccr/pdf/15/pdf/01507.pdf|naid=|doi=|ref=}} - リンクは東京文化財研究所。 * {{Cite journal|和書|author=滝沢誠|title=長野県松本市桜ケ丘古墳の再調査|date=1988-10|journal=信濃|publisher=信濃史学会|volume=40|number=10|pages=941-954|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Sakuragaoka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html 桜ヶ丘古墳出土品] - 松本市ホームページ * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2111 桜ヶ丘古墳出土品] - 公益財団法人八十二文化財団 {{デフォルトソート:さくらかおかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]]

'''Sakuragaoka Kofun''' (桜ヶ丘古墳, also known as 桜ケ丘古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Asama Onsen|ja|浅間温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Matsumoto|ja|松本市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - Designated on October 18, 2010. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Matsumoto City Archaeological Museum|ja|松本市立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nakayama, Matsumoto City) - Houses and displays artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/soshiki/134/3725.html Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - Matsumoto City Official Website * [https://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2111 Artifacts from Sakuragaoka Kofun] - 82 Cultural Property Foundation Horned Kabuto 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Sakuragaoka Kofun|ja|桜ヶ丘古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 甲冑.JPG|Armor (Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property) 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Helmet with Horn-like Protrusion 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 短甲.JPG|Short Armor 桜ヶ丘古墳 出土品.JPG|Ornaments, Weapons, and Neck Armor == Cultural Property Designation == === Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property === * **Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property (Tangible Cultural Property)** ** Artifacts excavated from Sakuragaoka Kofun (archaeological materials) - It is a {{ill|empun (circular tumulus)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun (circular tumulus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}. Although not designated as a historic site, its excavated artifacts are designated as {{ill|Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|ja|長野県宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property|qq=}}. == Overview == This kofun is located at the tip of a ridge in the Sakuragaoka hills on the northeastern edge of the {{ill|Matsumoto Basin|ja|松本盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Basin|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 678 meters. It was discovered in {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1954|qq=}} by junior high school students and excavated in {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1955|qq=}}, yielding numerous {{ill|grave goods|ja|副葬品|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/grave goods|qq=}}. The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical pit stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tenkan (crown)|qq=}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/armor|qq=}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1954|qq=}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1955|qq=}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1966|qq=}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oba Iwao|qq=}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1987|qq=}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1988|qq=}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1988|qq=}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. * {{ill|2002|ja|2002nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}: Reorganization of artifacts by the Matsumoto City Board of Education, with the report published in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. * {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} (October 18): Artifacts designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. == Artifacts == 桜ヶ丘古墳出土 金銅製天冠.JPG|Gilt-bronze Tenkan (Nagano The tumulus is circular, measuring approximately 30 meters in diameter and about 5 meters in height{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The burial facility, though poorly preserved, is a type of {{ill|vertical pit stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical pit stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, divided into a main chamber and a sub-chamber{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. The main chamber measures 2.5 meters in length and 1.2–1.3 meters in width, while the sub-chamber (stone cist) measures 1.8–1.85 meters in length and about 0.6 meters in width{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. Numerous grave goods, including a gilt-bronze {{ill|tenkan (crown)|ja|天冠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tenkan (crown)|qq=}} and a full set of {{ill|armor|ja|甲冑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/armor|qq=}}, were discovered inside the stone chamber. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}, during the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}{{sfn|Nagano Prefecture Matsumoto City Sakuragaoka Kofun Reorganization Report|2003}}. It is unique because it contains both symbols of political authority (such as the tenkan and combs) and military power (such as weapons and armor). The artifacts were designated as Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}}. == Site History == * {{ill|1954|ja|1954年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1954|qq=}}: Discovered by students from Metoba Junior High School. * {{ill|1955|ja|1955年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1955|qq=}}: Excavation conducted, yielding grave goods (reported in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1966|qq=}} by researchers including {{ill|Oba Iwao|ja|大場磐雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oba Iwao|qq=}} of {{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}}). * {{ill|1969|ja|1969年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1969|qq=}}: The gilt-bronze tenkan designated as a Nagano Prefecture Cultural Property. * {{ill|1987|ja|1987年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1987|qq=}}: Mound measurement survey and re-measurement of some artifacts (reported in {{ill|1988|ja|1988年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1988|qq=}} by Makoto Takizawa and others). * {{ill|1988|ja|1988年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1988|qq=}} (March 17): Artifacts other than the gilt-bronze tenkan designated as Matsumoto City Important Cultural Property. *
